reason: Gateway attempt/gate changes must follow the backend pre-development checklist.
Managed aliases must keep one provider candidate while preserving same-provider retry and common gate semantics.
Feature crosses Rust, generated bindings, frontend queries/UI, config migration and request-log presentation.
Profile work must coexist with existing Codex config ownership and generated type boundaries.

Provider UUID creation must preserve additive disabled share-import semantics and exclude local models/profiles.
Bundle v4 must preserve independent version thresholds, validation-before-mutation and rollback behavior.
Config import changes must stay inside the existing lock/CAS/rollback ownership model.
Reuse existing URL, bounded body, atomic file and credential helpers instead of parallel implementations.
Defines the required typed data flow and generated boundary checks for this cross-layer feature.

Existing provider CRUD, model discovery, gateway and config ownership evidence.
Existing provider editor, Codex model catalog, query and UI integration points.
Protocol-specific model-list behavior, limits and OpenAI-compatible MVP boundary.

Provider UUID lifecycle, config import, share/duplicate and profile ownership evidence.
